title: Rustic Moroccan Bread Flatbread
description: Winey, flaky flatbread infused with cloves and long-fermented dough, ideal for festive Moroccan gatherings.
instructions: In a large bowl, dissolve 7g yeast and 5g sugar in 320ml warm water and let stand for 5 minutes until frothy.
Stir in 500g flour, 10g salt, 2g cloves, 1g caraway, 20ml olive oil, 100ml red wine, and 50g plain yogurt until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise at room temperature for 3 hours, then refrigerate overnight for long fermentation.
Remove dough from fridge, let rest 30 minutes at room temperature, then divide into 4 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin circle about 12 cm diameter and bake on a preheated baking stone at 220°C for 12 minutes per side until golden.
Cool on a rack before serving; optionally drizzle with olive oil or serve with harissa dip.
Store leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
